title: Form use to Survey Patients - redirect pop-up
excerpt: We’ve been using a Monday.com Form to survey patients after their appointments, accessed via a tablet outside the waiting room. Previously, we used a URL redirect so that after each submission, the form would automatical…

posts.author: ecarmina (Erin Carmina)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’ve been using a Monday.com Form to survey patients after their appointments, accessed via a tablet outside the waiting room. Previously, we used a URL redirect so that after each submission, the form would automatically reload for the next patient.

Recently, however, this behavior changed. Now, a redirect notice pop-up appears, and unfortunately, the language in that pop-up can’t be customized. To work around this, we removed the URL redirect and enabled the “Allow multiple submissions” setting. This now shows a “Submit another form” button after each submission.

While we can customize the message that appears above the button to clearly prompt the next patient for feedback, the button text itself can’t be changed—which feels limiting. It seems like there should be a better solution, but I haven’t found one yet.

We’re planning to test this setup to see if it’s confusing for patients, but I’m hopeful someone has discovered a more effective workaround. Thanks in advance!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
What does the pop up say?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: ecarmina (Erin Carmina)
posts.cookedHtml:
It states that you are being redirected, and then has the URL and asks if you are sure you want to continue.

Screenshot 2025-08-28 144320590×298 20.8 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Got it! Yea, I don’t think that is ‘editable’ due to privacy rules with website redirections. I’d recommend using the ‘submit another’. I’ve used that in the past and not had confusion from users.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ecarmina

Our monday app Easyform supports redirects without any popups getting in the way. In addition to that, all buttons on the form are editable and allow you to change them to any language.

You can check out our documentation here. If you want help setting up or have any questions, you can reach us at easyformsupport@easyapphq.com or book a call with us here.

posts.author: SariHoltz (Sari Holtz)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Erin! SuperForm allows you to redirect to a new page without this message.
